issues: metadata.image_paths and top-level image_paths refer to different image paths.

image_dependency: question_text explicitly refers to 'the figure' and compares solid vs dashed waveforms at two times
multi_step: solving requires combining waveform snapshots at t=0 and t=0.01 s with the frequency constraint f < 30 Hz
second sub-question asks both first downward equilibrium-crossing time and average velocity magnitude for particle P, indicating sequential reasoning from graph-derived phase information
verifiability: answer_text gives a compact set of checkable outputs: speed, direction, time, and average-velocity magnitude
the expected outputs are numerical/closed-form plus a discrete direction, which are suitable for rule-based or physics-based checking

metadata.image_paths and top-level image_paths refer to different image paths.

Reported issues are metadata inconsistencies about image paths and answer type, but they do not remove the recoverable problem content.

sentence_segments.text: A transverse simple harmonic wave propagates along the x-axis.
The solid and dashed lines in the figure represent the waveforms at times $t = 0$ and $t = 0.01 \, \text{s}$, respectively, within the region $0 \leqslant \mathbf{x} \leqslant 8 \, \text{m}$.
It is known that the frequency of the wave is $\mathrm{f} < 30 \, \text{Hz}$.
The magnitude of the wave speed and the direction of wave propagation
In the figure, particle P (coordinates $\mathbf{x_p} = 1\mathrm{m}$) starts at time $t=0$.
How long does it take for P to pass the equilibrium position downward for the first time, and what is the magnitude of the average velocity of P during this process?

normalization_notes: Question explicitly depends on the figure to compare the solid and dashed waveforms at two times.
Force flag indicates image is required, so multimodal processing is retained.
No choice options were provided; choice map kept empty.
Answer text preserved with only minimal formatting cleanup.
